nativeRange.habitat: Origin uncertain; commonly described as a hybrid/cultivar of horticultural origin, likely involving Cryptocoryne wendtii lineage. The genus Cryptocoryne is native to tropical streams, rivers, and seasonally inundated forest pools across India, Southeast Asia, and New Guinea.

decorPreferences: root tabs near root zone for iron supplementation
high light to develop intense pink/coral coloration
stable parameters to minimize crypt melt

avoidWith: Silver Dollars and other plant-eating fish

summary: A rare, highly prized Cryptocoryne hybrid/cultivar with dramatic pink and coral leaf coloration. Considered a specialty/premium planted-tank plant; generally advised to provide high light, soft water, iron-rich fertilization, and very stable parameters to develop and hold the signature pink color.
careNotes: Cryptocoryne 'Pink Flamingo' is a rare specialty cultivar in the planted-tank hobby, prized for its distinctive ruffled leaves that emerge blood-red and transition to vibrant pink and coral tones — a coloration unusual in the otherwise green/brown/bronze Cryptocoryne genus. Its precise taxonomic origin is somewhat uncertain in the hobby literature: it is most commonly described as a hybrid or designer cultivar likely derived from the Cryptocoryne wendtii lineage (Buce Plant lists Borneo as the origin; this is the same regional attribution they apply to many Crypts and should be treated with caution given Wikipedia documents the genus as Indian/Southeast Asian/New Guinean). Mature plants form compact rosettes, generally on the smaller end of the Cryptocoryne range — typically around 5-15 cm tall — making this a midground (occasionally foreground) specimen rather than a large background rosette. To achieve and hold the signature pink coloration, the prevailing hobby advice is HIGH lighting (low light tends to produce washed-out, greener leaves), supplemental CO2 (not strictly required but strongly recommended for color intensity and growth rate), iron-rich fertilization (iron-dosed root tabs and water-column micronutrients are widely cited as critical for the pink pigment), and soft-to-moderately-soft water. Like other Cryptocorynes, this plant is a HEAVY ROOT FEEDER — nutrient uptake happens primarily through the root zone, so a nutrient-rich substrate (aqua soil) and regular root tabs are strongly recommended. Plant by burying the roots in substrate up to the crown (the point where leaves emerge from the root mass); do NOT bury the crown itself. CRYPT MELT WARNING: Pink Flamingo is reported to be more sensitive to crypt melt than common Crypts like C. wendtii — newly planted specimens or those exposed to a sudden parameter shift frequently shed most or all of their leaves and may take 3-6 weeks (sometimes longer) to recover. Stable parameters and avoiding relocation once planted are essential. Cross-source confirmation was limited: Buce Plant (primary) confirms pH 6-7, low-to-medium light tolerated but pink color requires more, propagation by rhizome cutting, and 'easy once established' difficulty. Aquarium Co-Op's product page returned 404 (likely discontinued listing or URL change), and Tropica/Aquasabi pages did not surface during this research. Wikipedia provided genus-level taxonomy and tolerance ranges only. Lower-stakes fields (temperature 22-28 °C, gH 2-15, kH 1-10, tank size, decor preferences, compatibility lists) are inherited from the Cryptocoryne wendtii baseline (fw-plant-024); these are biologically reasonable for a wendtii-lineage cultivar but were not directly confirmed for the Pink Flamingo variety. Set to needs_review because cross-source verification of high-stakes fields was thin.
breedingNotes: Propagated primarily by rhizome cutting/division (Buce Plant explicitly notes 'cut by rhizome') — divide a healthy rhizome into sections, each retaining leaves and roots, and replant. Runner (stolon) production typical of other Cryptocorynes is also reported but is reportedly slower for this cultivar than for vigorous species like C. wendtii. Seed propagation is not practical in the home aquarium.

primary.name: Buce Plant
primary.notes: Primary product page. Confirmed pH 6-7, low-to-medium light, CO2 optional but recommended, midground placement, rhizome propagation, 'easy once established' difficulty. Lists native range as Borneo (treated with caution — the same blanket attribution is used for other Crypts where it is incorrect). Page is thin on temperature, gH/kH, and adult size.

additional.name: Wikipedia (Cryptocoryne genus)
additional.notes: Genus-level cross-check: confirmed Araceae/Alismatales taxonomy, broad native range across India/Southeast Asia/New Guinea (does not corroborate Buce Plant's Borneo-specific attribution), tolerance temperature 12-33 °C, and the crypt-melt phenomenon. Wikipedia does not document Pink Flamingo specifically.

additional.name: Inheritance baseline: Cryptocoryne wendtii (fw-plant-024)
additional.notes: Lower-stakes fields (temperature 22-28 °C, gH 2-15, kH 1-10, decor preferences, compatibility lists, minimum tank size) inherited from the sister/parent species C. wendtii entry, which was researched against Tropica/Aquasabi/Aquarium Co-Op/Wikipedia/AquariumPlants.com. Inheritance is documented in careNotes; reviewer should verify these baseline values are appropriate for the Pink Flamingo cultivar specifically.
